old_claim.note: Jet Engine is modeled as a necessary component or method for this technology in the current graph.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency edge remains from rocketry to the local jet_engine node; rocketry stays modeled through propellants, chemistry, thermodynamics, construction, mathematics, and gunpowder instead.

source_shape.source_locator: Rocket Engines and Their Propellants section contrasting jet engines with rockets and describing solid and liquid rocket engines.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: NASA distinguishes jet engines, which use oxygen from surrounding air, from rockets, which must carry oxidizer for operation where there is no air; the same page describes solid and liquid rocket engines and their propellants.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This rejects the hard dependency because the local jet_engine node is explicitly airbreathing, while the rocketry node is about reaction-engine propulsion for high altitude and space and should not require an airbreathing engine.

ontology_before: Rocketry incorrectly depended on an airbreathing jet_engine node as a required prerequisite, conflating aircraft jet propulsion with rocket propulsion.
ontology_after: Rocketry no longer has a direct jet_engine prerequisite; rocket propulsion remains connected to propellants, chemistry, thermodynamics, construction, mathematics, and gunpowder.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Invariant preserved: rocketry still has chemistry, propellant, historical gunpowder, thermodynamic, mathematical, and construction context. Changed: airbreathing jet engines are no longer treated as a hard prerequisite.
would_reject_if: The local jet_engine node were redefined to mean any reaction engine, including rocket engines, instead of an airbreathing engine.
A source showed that the scoped broad rocketry technology historically or technically requires aircraft-style airbreathing jet engines.
A future graph split introduced a precise rocket_engine node and the removed edge was replaced by that more accurate dependency with source support.
short_reason: Rocketry should not require airbreathing jet engines; NASA's practical rocketry material separates rocket engines from jet engines by oxidizer source and operating environment.
